title: Song Familiarity
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 29 participants performing a song familiarity recognition task. Participants listened to song melodies and indicated when songs became familiar, followed by identification attempts and multiple-choice recognition with visual feedback. The study investigates neural correlates of music familiarity and recognition processes using synchronized EEG and audio recordings.
methods_description: Participants listened to song melodies presented as audio stimuli and responded when songs felt familiar. Following familiarity detection, participants attempted to identify songs by title, artist, or lyrics. A multiple-choice recognition phase presented four song titles with visual feedback (green checkmark for correct, red X for incorrect selections). EEG was recorded synchronously with audio stimuli, with reconstructed audio files available in the derivatives folder.
